name: Kritavarma
description: Kritavarma, a Yadava warrior, was a prominent figure in the Mahabharata. He was a commander in the Narayani Sena, a powerful army belonging to Krishna's Yadava clan. Known for his martial prowess and loyalty, Kritavarma fought on the Kaurava side during the Kurukshetra War. He was a skilled archer and warrior, respected for his courage and battle strategy. Despite his association with the losing side, Kritavarma was not inherently evil but rather bound by his commitment to Duryodhana. He played a crucial role in several key battles, demonstrating his unwavering dedication to his chosen allegiance. He survived the war, becoming one of the few Kaurava warriors to do so. However, his survival was short-lived, as he met his end during the Yadava fratricide described in the Mausala Parva of the Mahabharata. His story serves as a complex exploration of dharma, loyalty, and the tragic consequences of war. Kritavarma's decision to fight for the Kauravas, despite knowing their अधर्म (adharma) or unrighteousness, provides a nuanced perspective on the ethical dilemmas faced by warriors in ancient Indian epics. He is a reminder that heroes are not always purely good or evil, but often individuals caught in the web of circumstances and obligations.
subtitle: Yadava Warrior, Kaurava Ally

keyMyths.title: Kurukshetra War Allegiance
keyMyths.description: Kritavarma chose to fight for the Kauravas, aligning himself with Duryodhana due to prior obligations and loyalty. This decision placed him against Krishna and the Pandavas, shaping his role in the war.

keyMyths.title: Narayani Sena Command
keyMyths.description: As a commander in the Narayani Sena, Kritavarma led a formidable army on the Kaurava side. His leadership and tactical skills were crucial in several battles.

keyMyths.title: Yadava Fratricide
keyMyths.description: Kritavarma's life ended during the Yadava fratricide, a consequence of the curse placed upon the Yadava clan. This event marked the end of his warrior journey.

sources.author: Vyasa
sources.description: The primary source for Kritavarma's story, detailing his role in the Kurukshetra War and his eventual demise.
